message:
docs(conformance): document DISC-013 + DISC-014 series-add divergences (br-9seu / fd90.81)

Trace through live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled
surfaced 2 underlying FP-P2C-001 series_add packet failures:

DISC-013: Series + Series union alignment does not sort the
result index. Pandas Series.add returns a sorted index for
differently-indexed operands; our impl preserves discovery order
(left labels first, then right-only appended). Surfaces as
actual=[1,3,2] vs expected=[1,2,3]. WILL-FIX coordinated slice
across fp-index align_union + fp-frame series arithmetic.

DISC-014: Series + Series with duplicate labels doesn't promote
Int64 to Float64. Pandas promotes the result dtype to Float64
because duplicate-label cross-product can introduce NaN, even
when the specific input avoids it. We preserve Int64 when no
NaN is actually generated. Inverse of DISC-011: there pandas
keeps Int64 where we promote; here pandas promotes where we
keep. Both stem from the absence of a nullable extension Int64
dtype. WILL-FIX coordinated with the DISC-011 epic.

Now all 5 of the known fp-conformance failures have explicit
DISC traceability:

  - FP-P2D-028 dataframe_concat_axis1_packet              -> DISC-011
  - FP-P2D-433 dataframe_to_json_records_packet           -> DISC-011
  - fuzz_json_io_bytes_accepts_records_seed_fixture       -> DISC-011
  - FP-P2D-429 csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone  -> DISC-012
  - live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back (FP-P2C-001 series add)
                                                          -> DISC-013 + DISC-014

Pure documentation. Build passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -213,6 +213,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9p0u","title":"fp-types Timestamp struct + Timedelta arithmetic (4r56 Phase 2)","description":"Phase 2 of 4r56 (Timedelta arithmetic shipped in commit 68e2087).

**Scope.** Add a `Timestamp` struct to fp-types providing:
- Nanosecond-precision i64 storage (Unix epoch anchored).
- NaT sentinel (i64::MIN) for missingness.
- Arithmetic: `Timestamp + Timedelta -> Timestamp`, `Timestamp - Timestamp -> Timedelta`, `Timestamp - Timedelta -> Timestamp`.
- Saturation on overflow; NaT propagation.
- Serde round-trip + semantic_eq.

**Non-scope (deferred to Phase 3).**
- TZ-aware Timestamp: a `tz: Option<String>` field is reserved now, but TZ-dependent arithmetic (DST transitions, tz conversion via chrono_tz) lands in a separate slice that pulls chrono_tz into fp-types's dep graph.
- ISO 8601 parsing / strftime display: chrono integration stays in downstream crates; Phase 2 ships constructor-from-nanos only.
- Display: Phase 2 uses a debug-ish format `"Timestamp[<nanos>, UTC]"`; pandas notation lands in Phase 3.

**Blast radius.** Purely additive in fp-types (no existing call-site touched). Zero dep-graph change (no chrono in fp-types yet).

**Tests (10):**
- Construction from nanos.
- Timestamp + Timedelta arithmetic.
- Timestamp - Timestamp = Timedelta.
- Timestamp - Timedelta arithmetic.
- NaT propagation (Timestamp or Timedelta is NaT).
- Saturation on overflow.
- semantic_eq NaT-aware.
- Ordering (non-NaT comparison respects chronological order).
- Serde round-trip.
- Compile-time Send+Sync assertion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:23:53.821472519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:26:20.881490680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:26:20.879041374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","review","timestamp"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9q98","title":"[HIGH] REVIEW: mixed naive/offset to_datetime utc parity drift","description":"Found while building frankenpandas-ow57 datetime conformance matrix.

Failing input: series_to_datetime, datetime_utc=true, values=["2024-01-15 10:30:00", "2024-01-15 10:30:00+05:30"].

Expected from live pandas oracle: ["2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00", null].

Actual from FrankenPandas: ["2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00", "2024-01-15 05:00:00+00:00"].

Impact: pandas inferred-format coercion rejects the offset-aware second value in this mixed naive/offset sequence, while FrankenPandas accepts and normalizes it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T06:06:41.985402560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:41:32.596622149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:41:32.594733021Z","close_reason":"Fixed to_datetime mixed naive/offset UTC parity drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datetime","parity","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9s0g","title":"fd90.65: fp-frame needs crate-level rustdoc summarizing the user-facing surface","description":"Continuation of fd90.61-64 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-frame - the largest workspace crate (64K lines) and most user-facing one. Public surface includes DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling/Expanding/EWM, Resample, accessors (Categorical/Sparse/String/Datetime), to_datetime/to_timedelta/to_numeric coercion fns, concat_*, plus the merge/join helpers. Doc summarizes the major user-facing types organized by area: core types, group-by/window operations, accessors, type coercion, frame combination. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:59:39.250764334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:02:38.261550285Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:02:38.257565231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-9seu","title":"fd90.81: DISC-013 - series add union alignment doesn't sort result index","description":"Trace through live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled failure surfaced 2 underlying FP-P2C-001 series_add packet failures: (1) series_add_alignment_union_strict index mismatch: actual=[1,3,2], expected=[1,2,3] - our union alignment doesn't sort the result index; pandas sorts. (2) series_add_duplicate_labels_hardened value mismatch: actual=Int64(4), expected=Float64(4.0) - inverse of DISC-011: pandas promotes Int64 to Float64 when duplicate-label cross-product introduces NaN, our impl keeps Int64. Document as DISC-013 (sort) + extend DISC-011 (inverse Int64-vs-Float64 case where pandas does the promotion we don't). Pure docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:08:04.792238401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:10:02.274381489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:10:02.271657337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discrepancy","docs","fd90","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-9uuq","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling std|var","description":"Add live pandas oracle coverage for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling(window).std() and var() with ddof=1 semantics, interleaved groups, and windows that become invalid due to missing values. Log parity artifacts under phase2c so failures show exact bucket-local drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:29:38.729141183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:40:51.995865875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:40:51.993469499Z","close_reason":"shipped 6edb388: 2 fp-frame tests (groupby_rolling_std_var_int64_input_widens_to_float_with_ddof1_semantics, groupby_rolling_std_var_all_nan_window_emits_nan) complementary to x5oy's float-input baseline. Locks Int64->Float64 dtype coercion + ddof=1 sample-variance numerics + all-NaN window NaN-emission. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9v09","title":"pandas: categorical value_counts category-awareness parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:00:22.291998183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:49:49.740823568Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:49:49.738989716Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -77,6 +77,22 @@
 - **Tests affected:** `dataframe_groupby_apply`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_scalar_returns_series_indexed_by_keys`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_series_unions_sparse_result_columns`, `dataframe_groupby_apply_series_stacked_preserves_variable_labels`.
 - **Review date:** 2026-04-25
 
+### DISC-013: Series + Series union alignment does not sort the result index
+- **Reference:** Pandas `Series.add(other)` (and `series + other` operator) on differently-indexed Series performs an outer-join alignment that returns a sorted result index by default.
+- **Our impl:** fp-frame's series-add union alignment preserves discovery order (left labels first, then right-only labels appended) rather than sorting. So `Series([1,2], index=[1,3]) + Series([10,20], index=[1,2])` yields a result indexed `[1, 3, 2]` while pandas yields `[1, 2, 3]`.
+- **Impact:** Conformance packet `FP-P2C-001 series_add_alignment_union_strict` fails with `index mismatch: actual=[Int64(1), Int64(3), Int64(2)], expected=[Int64(1), Int64(2), Int64(3)]`. Downstream test `live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled` re-surfaces this via the FP-P2C-001 fallback fixture. Same mismatch will appear on any `+` / `-` / `*` / `/` between Series with non-matching indexes where the union order would differ.
+- **Resolution:** WILL-FIX - implementing sorted union alignment in fp-index's `align_union` (and the SeriesArithmetic dispatcher in fp-frame) is a non-trivial behavior change that could affect any downstream code relying on discovery-order alignment. Tracked as a future fp-index/fp-frame coordinated slice. Per br-frankenpandas-9seu (fd90.81).
+- **Tests affected:** `packet_filter_runs::FP-P2C-001/series_add_alignment_union_strict`, `live_oracle_harness_availability::live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled` (the latter wraps the former via the fallback report).
+- **Review date:** 2026-04-26
+
+### DISC-014: Series + Series duplicate-label arithmetic doesn't promote Int64 to Float64
+- **Reference:** Pandas `Series + Series` with duplicate labels on either side performs cross-product alignment that can introduce NaN for the pairings with no match. Pandas promotes the result to `Float64` to accommodate the NaN even when both sources are pure `Int64` and the actual numeric result fits in `Int64`.
+- **Our impl:** Our cross-product alignment preserves `Int64` when no NaN is actually generated (the duplicate-label paired values all match). This is the inverse of DISC-011: there pandas keeps Int64 (extension dtype) where we promote to Float64; here pandas promotes to Float64 where we keep Int64. Both stem from the absence of a nullable extension Int64 dtype on our side.
+- **Impact:** Conformance packet `FP-P2C-001 series_add_duplicate_labels_hardened` fails with `value mismatch at idx=0: actual=Int64(4), expected=Float64(4.0)`. Downstream test `live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled` re-surfaces this via the FP-P2C-001 fallback fixture.
+- **Resolution:** WILL-FIX - aligned with the DISC-011 nullable-extension-Int64 epic. The fix is "always promote to Float64 when duplicate-label alignment can introduce NaN, regardless of whether the specific input avoids it" — that's how pandas does it pre-extension-dtype too. Per br-frankenpandas-9seu (fd90.81).
+- **Tests affected:** `packet_filter_runs::FP-P2C-001/series_add_duplicate_labels_hardened`, `live_oracle_harness_availability::live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled`.
+- **Review date:** 2026-04-26
+
 ### DISC-012: Mixed naive / tz-aware CSV parse_dates bails out and returns raw input strings
 - **Reference:** Pandas handles a CSV column with mixed naive + tz-aware datetime strings by parsing each row independently (the naive rows produce `Timestamp` without tz; the aware rows produce `Timestamp` with tz). When converted to strings, both forms are reformatted into pandas' canonical `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS[±HH:MM]` shape.
 - **Our impl:** fp-frame's `to_datetime_with_options(infer_mixed_timezone=true)` infers ONE tz pattern (Naive or Aware) from the FIRST non-null row. Rows that don't match that pattern are coerced to `NaT`. fp-io's `parse_csv_datetime_column` then sees the partial-parse failure and returns `None`, leaving the entire column as the raw input strings. So the second row in a mixed-tz column keeps its original `2024-01-15T10:30:00Z` form even though our `format_aware_datetime` would have rendered it correctly as `2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00`.
